name: Aosta Valley (Italy)

colors.symbolism: the hoist half, taken from the medieval arms of the city of Aosta
the fly half, taken from the medieval arms of the city of Aosta

description: A vertical bicolour with a black band at the hoist and a red band at the fly, in equal proportions.
history: Adopted by Regional Law n. 1 of 16 March 2006, codifying a flag of black-and-red, the historic livery of the city of Aosta and of the medieval Valdotain Challant family. The pairing of black and red has identified the valley since at least the 13th century.

features.symbolism: A vertical bicolour of black at the hoist and red at the fly, derived from the historic livery colours of the House of Challant and the medieval banner of the city of Aosta.
